name: Crooker v. California
question: Is a confession obtained during an interrogation in which the suspect asked for and was denied access to counsel obtained in violation of the suspect’s Fourteenth Amendment right to due process?
description: A case in which the Court held that a confession given after the defendant’s specific request for counsel was denied does not violate the Due Process Clause of the Fourteenth Amendment when there was evidence that the defendant was aware of his rights.
